source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage, Crane/Other Lifting

actions_taken: Medivac helicopter transport of two injured employees
BSEE onsite investigation conducted on May 19, 2018
Evidence collection, photographs, and witness statements gathered
event.summary: On May 19, 2018, while rigging down blow out preventers (BOPs) and high pressure riser on the A-8 well aboard the Holstein Spar Rig, a lateral support guide frame (LSG) fell approximately 17 feet after two employees removed bolts securing the frame to the riser. The crew removed all nuts from sixteen studs contrary to the planned procedure and the Driller's instructions. The Toolpusher, newly promoted and unfamiliar with the procedure, instructed the crew to remove the bolts before the LSG frame was properly supported. Both employees were injured and evacuated by medivac.
key_phrases: Failure to follow the planned procedure
Disregard of experienced crew member instructions

consequences.name: Equipment damage to lateral support guide frame

controls.name: Job Safety Analysis (JSA)
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to ensure procedure awareness

supporting_text: Prior to commencing work, a Job Safety Analysis (JSA) was reviewed and signed by the crew.
Attached to the JSA was a four page, step by step procedure to nipple down the BOP's and the high pressure riser.
the Toolpusher had not read the JSA or the procedure prior to the incident. He had signed the documentation, but did not actually read the documents until after the incident.

controls.name: Driller Instructions and Supervision
controls.barrier_role: Direct supervision and procedural guidance from experienced personnel

supporting_text: The Driller met with the drill crew that was performing this task on the BOP deck and gave instructions on how to complete the procedure, up to connecting the lifting cap on the high pressure riser. He also instructed the crew to stop once the lifting cap was secured.
the crew disregarded his instructions to not remove any bolts because the Toolpusher instructed them to do so.
the Driller was the only member of the crew that had experience with this task, but the crew disregarded his instructions

controls.name: Procedure Step Sequencing and Load Support
controls.barrier_role: Engineering control ensuring proper sequence of operations

supporting_text: The correct procedure for removing the riser from the LSG frame would have been to bolt the lifting cap onto the riser, release the riser from the wellhead, lift the riser and LSG frame until the lateral support dogs could be pulled into place, pull out the support dogs, lower the riser and LSG frame onto the beams at which point the LSG and Riser would be supported, remove the nuts from the studs holding the riser to the LSG, and pull the riser out of the LSG frame.

At the Toolpusher's instruction, the crew unbolted the nuts from the studs holding the riser to the LSG before the LSG frame was supported by the dogs. The failure to follow the planned procedure triggered the incident

controls.name: Work Stop Authority and Procedure Compliance
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control requiring crew to stop work when instructions deviate from procedure

supporting_text: Failure to stop work when instruction was given that was not in line with the planned procedure.

one of the Floorhands involved with the procedure told the rest of the crew that the Driller had instructed them to only install the lifting cap on the riser and to not remove any bolts. He told the crew that they shouldn't be removing the bolts, but they proceeded regardless.
According to interviews, the crew felt that since the Toolpusher was the Drillers supervisor, they must follow his orders as he must have known what needed to be done.

controls.name: Toolpusher Competency and Procedure Familiarization
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control ensuring supervisory personnel are competent and familiar with procedures

supporting_text: the Toolpusher had not read the JSA or the procedure prior to the incident. He had signed the documentation, but did not actually read the documents until after the incident.
He had been promoted from Deck Foreman only five days prior to the incident, and had never been involved with this procedure before.

controls.name: Hazard Detection - Frame Shift Awareness
controls.barrier_role: Detection of structural instability during unbolting operations

supporting_text: It was later learned that this Floorhand had felt the LSG frame shift while it was being unbolted and he moved outside of the work area while the other Floorhand and Shakerhand continued unbolting the high pressure riser from the LSG frame.

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
